commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=143a3b015527b63e3110728a3cbf6b92be9b7417)
commands.review_summary:
The branch records AWS SSH CIDRs on lease records and uses active AWS lease CIDRs when creating or refreshing shared security-group ingress, with focused tests and docs updates.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sends only the current request CIDR to AWS ingress refresh, and the AWS provider prunes managed SSH rules outside that desired set. I did not run live AWS reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer review of the upgrade-safe AWS ingress path before merge; ClawSweeper repair should not run while proof is missing.

target.title: fix(worker): preserve active AWS SSH ingress
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes AWS SSH ingress churn by treating active lease CIDRs as fleet state instead of letting each new launch or heartbeat prune the shared security group down to only its own request source.

The regression was visible when one AWS lease opened SSH successfully, then another AWS lease refreshed `crabbox-runners` from a different source IP and removed the first lease's still-needed CIDR during bootstrap.

## Verification

- `npm test --prefix worker -- fleet.test.ts`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: obviyus
